data.title: Repare Therapeutics Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Repare Therapeutics Inc., a leading clinical-stage precision oncology company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and key business developments, underscoring significant strides in revenue generation and strategic realignments.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $11.6 million, driven by the Debiopharm License Agreement.

Income (loss) from operations: $1.1 million, reflecting gains from the termination of the Debiopharm collaboration agreement.

Net income (loss): $3.3 million, compared to a loss of $34.4 million in the prior year period, primarily due to revenue from the Debiopharm License Agreement and cost reductions.

Basic Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $0.08, compared to a loss of $0.81 per share in the prior year period.

Diluted Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $0.08, reflecting the positive net income achieved in the current period.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company recognized revenue from the Debiopharm License Agreement, which contributed significantly to the revenue for the period. This agreement involved an exclusive worldwide license for the product candidate lunresertib.

Geographical Performance: Revenue was primarily derived from Switzerland due to the Debiopharm License Agreement, highlighting the importance of this region in the company's operations.

New Product Launches: The company entered into an exclusive worldwide license agreement with Debiopharm for lunresertib, a first-in-class oncology PKMYT1 inhibitor, marking a significant development in their product offerings.

Future Outlook: The company expects to maintain a minimal workforce to finalize the transaction with XenoTherapeutics, Inc., which is anticipated to close in the first quarter of 2026. This strategic move is expected to impact the company's operational focus and resource allocation.

Strategic Re-Prioritization: The company announced a re-alignment of resources and a re-prioritization of its clinical portfolio to focus on the advancement of its Phase 1 clinical programs, RP-3467 and RP-1664, reflecting a shift in strategic focus.

Operational Adjustments: The company executed a phased reorganization plan to reduce its workforce by approximately 75% by the fourth quarter of 2025, aligning its resources with its strategic priorities.

Collaboration Termination: The Debiopharm Collaboration Agreement was terminated as part of the new license agreement, resulting in a gain on termination and reflecting a strategic shift in collaboration efforts.

SEC Filing: Repare Therapeutics Inc. [ RPTX ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: NEXTNRG, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
NEXTNRG, INC., a leading company in the sustainable energy and mobile fueling industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights substantial financial growth and strategic business initiatives aimed at expanding the company's market reach and operational capabilities.

Financial Highlights

Sales - net: NEXTNRG, INC. reported net sales of $58.82 million, a significant increase from $20.98 million in the same period last year. This growth was driven by expanded operations and increased customer demand.

Gross margin (loss): The company achieved a gross margin of $4.53 million, up from $1.62 million, indicating improved cost management and operational efficiency.

Loss from operations: The loss from operations was $(45.52) million, compared to $(7.45) million in the prior year period. This increase was primarily due to higher general and administrative expenses, including stock-based compensation.

Net loss: The net loss for the period was $(60.05) million, a substantial increase from $(18.84) million, impacted by higher interest expenses and loss on settlement of liabilities.

Basic and diluted loss per share: The basic and diluted loss per share was $(0.51), compared to $(0.18) in the previous year, reflecting the increased net loss and a higher weighted average number of shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Fuel sales accounted for 96.49% of total revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, indicating a strong focus on this product line.

Customer Concentration: A significant portion of sales, 48.85%, was attributed to a single customer, highlighting a potential risk in customer concentration.

Geographical Performance: The company continues to expand its national footprint, integrating sustainable energy solutions into its mobile fueling operations, which suggests a strategic focus on broadening market reach.

Sales Units: NEXTNRG, INC. sold 34 trucks in May 2025 and 35 trucks between August 4 and August 29, 2025, as part of its operational activities. These trucks were then leased back to support ongoing operations.

New Product Launches: The company is beginning to provide services as a renewable energy company, focusing on developing and deploying wireless electric vehicle charging technology integrated with battery storage and solar energy solutions.

New Production Launches: NEXTNRG, INC. executed an asset purchase agreement with Shell to acquire 73 vehicles and above-ground storage tanks, which were placed into service in January 2025, supporting its growth and expansion plan.

Future Outlook: The company plans to expand into new and existing markets, obtain additional financing for growth, and collaborate with other businesses for strategic opportunities, indicating a proactive approach to future growth and sustainability.

SEC Filing: NEXTNRG, INC. [ NXXT ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: GENERATION INCOME PROPERTIES, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Generation Income Properties, Inc., a real estate investment trust (REIT) specializing in acquiring and managing income-generating properties, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities for the period ending September 30, 2025.

Financial Highlights

Generation Income Properties, Inc. reported the following key financial metrics for the third quarter and the first nine months of 2025:

  • Total Revenue: $2.47 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting a slight increase from $2.40 million in the same period in 2024.
  • Total Revenue: $7.28 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $7.09 million in the same period in 2024.
  • Operating Loss: $(1.70) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1.37) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Operating Loss: $(5.55) million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(4.04) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Net Loss: $(1.72) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(2.10) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Net Loss: $(6.98) million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(5.44) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders: $(2.83) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(2.97) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders: $(9.98) million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(8.15) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Basic & Diluted Loss Per Share Attributable to Common Stockholders: $(0.52) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.55) in the same period in 2024.
  • Basic & Diluted Loss Per Share Attributable to Common Stockholders: $(1.83) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1.60) in the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

The company's operational performance and key business activities for the third quarter of 2025 include:

  • Revenue Segments: Generation Income Properties derives its rental income from long-term net leases, with significant contributions from tenants such as the General Services Administration, Dollar General, Pre-K, Kohl's, and exp U.S. Services, each accounting for more than 10% of rental revenue.
  • Geographical Performance: The company owns properties across various locations in the United States, including Norfolk, VA; Manteo, NC; Vacaville, CA; Tucson, AZ; and Maitland, FL, with notable tenant presence in these regions.
  • New Product Launches: On February 6, 2025, the company acquired three single-tenant retail properties in Sanford, FL, Cleveland, TN, and Kernersville, NC, leased to Zaxby's, Dollar General, and Tractor Supply Co., respectively, expanding its portfolio.
  • Real Estate Held for Sale: During the third quarter of 2025, the company approved a plan to sell three assets located in Grand Junction, CO; Plant City, FL; and Maitland, FL, with a combined cost basis of approximately $10.73 million.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on optimizing shareholder value and benefits through strategic initiatives, including refinancing, extending terms for preferred equity and loans, and divesting underperforming properties. A Special Committee has been appointed to evaluate opportunities to enhance shareholder value.

SEC Filing: GENERATION INCOME PROPERTIES, INC. [ GIPR ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Cineverse Corp. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Cineverse Corp., a leading entertainment company specializing in digital streaming and theatrical releases, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights for the three and six months ended September 30, 2025.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: Cineverse Corp. reported total revenue of $12.4 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, marking a 3% decrease compared to the same period in 2024. This decline was primarily due to lower licensing revenue. However, for the six months ended September 30, 2025, total revenue increased by 7% to $23.5 million, driven by growth in digital releases and subscription services.

Operating Loss: The company experienced an operating loss of $(5.4) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.9) million in the same period in 2024. This increase in operating loss was attributed to higher marketing and compensation expenses. For the six months ended September 30, 2025, the operating loss widened to $(9.1) million from $(3.6) million in the same period in 2024, due to similar factors.

Net Loss: Cineverse Corp. reported a net loss of $(5.5) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1.2) million in the same period in 2024. The net loss for the six months ended September 30, 2025, was $(9.1) million, up from $(4.3) million in the same period in 2024, primarily due to increased operating expenses.

Net Loss Per Share: The net loss per share was $(0.31) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.09) in the same period in 2024. For the six months ended September 30, 2025, the net loss per share was $(0.53), compared to $(0.29) in the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

Streaming and Digital Revenue: The streaming and digital segment saw a decrease in revenue for the three months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to lower licensing revenue. However, the six-month period experienced an increase in revenue driven by the digital release of 'Terrifier 3' and growth in subscription-based platforms.

Base Distribution Revenue: There was a notable increase in base distribution revenue for both the three and six months ended September 30, 2025. This growth was attributed to the theatrical release of 'Toxic Avenger' and the final theatrical revenue for 'Terrifier 3'.

Podcast and Other Revenue: This segment experienced a decline in revenue for both the three and six months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to lower podcast direct advertising. However, there was an increase in Matchpoint™ revenue.

Direct Operating Expenses: Direct operating expenses decreased for both the three and six months ended September 30, 2025. This reduction was driven by lower variable costs such as royalty expenses and platform fees, despite increased theatrical distribution fees related to 'Toxic Avenger'.

Selling, General and Administrative Expenses: These expenses increased significantly for both the three and six months ended September 30, 2025. The rise was due to higher compensation expenses from increased headcount, professional services, legal expenses, and marketing expenses related to 'Toxic Avenger'.

Depreciation and Amortization: Amortization expenses increased due to higher capitalized content costs, reflecting the company's investment in content development.

Future Outlook: Cineverse Corp. plans to continue investing in content development and acquisitions, expecting to obtain appropriate returns on these investments. Management is focused on increasing revenue from existing services and offering new services to improve income from operations.

SEC Filing: Cineverse Corp. [ CNVS ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Repare Therapeutics Agrees to be Acquired by XenoTherapeutics
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

Repare Therapeutics said it reached an agreement to be acquired by XenoTherapeutics, in a deal which will see shareholders receive cash and contingent value rights.

Under the terms of the deal, Repare Therapeutics shareholders will receive an estimated $1.82 per share of common stock they hold, as well as one CVR per common share. Repare shares closed the day's regular session at $1.65. Shares were trading 26% higher in the after-hours session, at $2.08.

The agreement will see each holder of the contingent value rights eligible to receive certain cash payments, including receivables received by Repare within ninety days following the closing, as well as a percentage of net proceeds received from the company's existing partnerships with Bristol-Myers Squibb, Debiopharm and DCx Biotherapeutics.

The deal will be implemented by way of court-approved plan of arrangement under Canada's Business Corporations Act, Repare said.

The deal is expected to close in the first quarter of next year.

The company also reported third-quarter earnings per share of 8 cents on revenue of $11.6 million. In the prior year period, the company reported a loss per share of 81 cents.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: CBOT soybeans, corn, wheat fall in USDA data aftermath
data.description:
By Renee Hickman

Chicago grains took a dive on Friday, following a closely watched U.S. government crop report and the release of export data that could provide clues into Chinese buying.

The most-active soybean contract on the Chicago Board of Trade fell 22-1/2 cents to $11.24-1/2 a bushel.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture put out its first global supply and demand outlook since September following the end of the 43-day U.S. government shutdown.

The USDA pegged the average 2025 U.S. corn yield at 186.0 bushels per acre, down from 186.7 on September 12. It lowered its production estimate to 16.752 billion bushels, from 16.814 billion a month earlier.

For soybeans, the USDA projected 2025 U.S. yield at 53.0 bpa, down from 53.5 bpa in September. Production was projected at 4.253 billion bushels, down from 4.301 billion bushels.

But corn and soybeans dropped as the market reacted to the new data, with the trade expecting deeper cuts from the agency.

"The corn yield did not come down nearly as much as the trade thought and ending stocks go up so overall supply is bearish," said Don Roose, president of U.S. Commodities.

Many market players were also expecting smaller soybean yield numbers, said Roose.

The agency also sent out a six-week summary of daily U.S. agricultural sales, offering some clarity on China's recent U.S. soybean buying.

USDA data showed exporters sold 100,000 metric tons of soybeans to China for 2025/2026 delivery on October 30. They sold 232,000 metric tons of soybeans to China for 2025/2026 on November 3.

Reuters has previously reported that China has begun modest purchases of U.S. farm goods, but Beijing has not confirmed the White House's announcement of 12 million metric tons of soybean purchases by year-end.

Corn lost 11-1/4 cents to $4.30-1/4 a bushel and wheat fell 10-3/4 cents to $5.41-1/2 a bushel.

data.title: From OpenAI to Google, firms channel billions into AI infrastructure as demand booms
data.description:
Alphabet's Google unit will invest $40 billion over the next two years to build three new data centers in Texas, joining peers aggressively spending to build infrastructure to support the surge in artificial intelligence applications.

The investments reflect how the global tech industry is rapidly scaling up its computing and energy capacity to power the next generation of AI tools and services.

Here is a list of multi-billion dollar AI, cloud and chip deals signed recently:

NVIDIA-BACKED GROUP AND ALIGNED DATA CENTERS

An investor group including BlackRock, Microsoft and Nvidia is buying U.S.-based Aligned Data Centers, one of the world's biggest data center operators with nearly 80 facilities, in a deal worth $40 billion.

GOOGLE AND TEXAS

Google will invest $40 billion in three new data centers in Texas through 2027. One of the data centers will be in Armstrong County, in the Texas panhandle, and the other two in Haskell County, a stretch of West Texas near Abilene.

The company is also continuing to invest in its existing Midlothian campus and Dallas cloud region, part of the company's global network of 42 cloud regions.

BROADCOM AND OPENAI

OpenAI has partnered with Broadcom to produce its first in-house artificial intelligence processors, the latest tie-up for the world's most valuable startup for computing power amid surging demand for its services.

AMD AND OPENAI

AMD agreed to supply artificial intelligence chips to OpenAI in a multi-year deal that would also give the ChatGPT creator the option to buy up to roughly 10% of the chipmaker.

NVIDIA AND OPENAI

Nvidia is set to invest up to $100 billion in OpenAI and supply it with data center chips, in a deal giving the chipmaker a financial stake in OpenAI. OpenAI is already an important customer for Nvidia.

META AND COREWEAVE

CoreWeave has signed a $14 billion agreement with Meta to supply computing power to the Facebook parent.

NVIDIA AND INTEL

Nvidia will invest $5 billion in Intel , giving it roughly 4% of the company after new shares are issued.

ORACLE AND META

Oracle is in talks with Meta for a multi-year cloud computing deal worth about $20 billion, underscoring the social media giant's drive to secure faster access to computing power.

ORACLE AND OPENAI

Oracle is reported to have signed one of the biggest cloud deals ever with OpenAI, under which the ChatGPT maker is expected to buy $300 billion in computing power from the company for about five years.

COREWEAVE AND NVIDIA

CoreWeave signed a $6.3 billion initial order with backer Nvidia, a deal that guarantees that the AI chipmaker will purchase any cloud capacity not sold to customers.

NEBIUS GROUP AND MICROSOFT

Nebius Group will provide Microsoft with GPU infrastructure capacity in a deal worth $17.4 billion over a five-year term.

META AND GOOGLE

Google struck a six-year cloud computing deal with Meta Platforms worth more than $10 billion, Reuters had reported in August.

INTEL AND SOFTBANK GROUP

Intel is getting a $2 billion capital injection from SoftBank Group , making the Japanese tech investor one of the top-10 shareholders of the troubled U.S. chipmaker.

TESLA AND SAMSUNG

Tesla signed a $16.5 billion deal to source chips from Samsung Electronics , with the EV maker's CEO Elon Musk saying that the South Korean tech giant's new chip factory in Texas would make Tesla's next-generation AI6 chip.

META AND SCALE AI

Meta took a 49% stake for about $14.3 billion in Scale AI and brought in its 28-year-old CEO, Alexandr Wang, to play a prominent role in the tech giant's artificial intelligence strategy.

GOOGLE AND WINDSURF

Google hired several key staff members from AI code generation startup Windsurf and will pay $2.4 billion in license fees as part of the deal to use some of Windsurf's technology under non-exclusive terms.

COREWEAVE AND OPENAI

CoreWeave signed a five-year contract worth $11.9 billion with OpenAI in March, before the Nvidia-backed startup's IPO.

STARGATE DATACENTER PROJECT

Stargate is a joint venture between SoftBank, OpenAI and Oracle to build data centers. The project was announced in January by U.S. President Donald Trump, who said that the companies would invest up to $500 billion to fund infrastructure for artificial intelligence.

AMAZON AND ANTHROPIC

Amazon.com pumped $4 billion into OpenAI competitor Anthropic, doubling its investment in the firm known for its GenAI chatbot Claude.

data.title: Kadestone Capital Corp. Reports Q3 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - November 14, 2025) - Kadestone Capital Corp.  (OTCQB: KDCCF) ("Kadestone" or the "Company"), a vertically integrated property company, today announced its financial results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025.

Financial Results

For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, the Company reported a net loss of $3,608,106, or $0.08 per share, compared to a net loss of $2,761,871, or $0.06 per share, for the same period in the prior year. The increased loss was primarily driven by operating expenses including salaries and wages of $1,396,715, consulting fees of $1,303,693, and interest expense of $765,662. These expenses were partially offset by income from associates totaling $729,271 and income from an investment in a mortgage fund amounting to $199,484.

Net cash used in operating activities also increased, rising to $3,789,439 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $2,606,385 in the prior year, reflecting the higher level of operational spending during the period.

The above unaudited financial information, including comparative information, is expressed in Canadian dollars and has been prepared in accordance with IFRS Accounting Standards, using the accounting policies and methods of application as described in notes 2 and 3 of the Company's audited consolidated financial statements for the years ended December 31, 2024, and 2023.

About Kadestone

Kadestone was established to pursue the investment in, acquisition, development and management of residential and commercial income producing properties, and procurement and sale of building materials within major urban centres and high-growth, emerging markets in Canada. The Company operates five complimentary business lines spanning building materials procurement and supply, property development and construction, construction finance, asset ownership and property management. These synergistic business lines have solidified Kadestone's vision to become a market leading vertically integrated property company. Additional information can be found at www.kadestone.com.

For further information, please contact David Negus, CFO, Kadestone Capital Corp., dnegus@kadestone.com, 604 671-8142

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD

(signed) "Brent Billey"

President, CEO and Director

Neither TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Cautionary Statement Regarding Forward-Looking Statements

Certain information in this press release, including, but not limited to, statements regarding the Company's objectives, goals and future plans, including the Company's ability to identify opportunities and secure additional investments in 2025 and the Company's vision to become a leading vertically integrated property company, may constitute forward looking information (collectively, "forward-looking statements"), which can be identified by the use of terms such as "may," "will," "should," "expect," "anticipate," "project," "estimate," "intend," "continue" or "believe" (or the negatives) or other similar variations. Because of various risks and uncertainties, including those referenced below, actual events or results may differ materially from those reflected or contemplated in such forward-looking statements. As a result, you should not rely on such forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements reflect material expectations and assumptions, including, without limitation, expectations and assumptions relating to Kadestone's ability to receive sufficient financing to execute its business objectives or plans on acceptable terms or at all; Kadestone's ability to realize the anticipated benefits for its synergistic business lines; and the stability of the financial and capital markets. Additional information identifying assumptions, risks and uncertainties relating to Kadestone is contained in Kadestone's filings with the Canadian securities regulators available at www.sedarplus.ca. These risks include, but are not limited to, Kadestone's requirement of significant additional capital; Kadestone's ability to receive sufficient financing to execute its business objectives or plans on acceptable terms or at all; and those other risks and uncertainties described in the "Risk Factors" section of the Company's final prospectus dated September 2, 2020, and in the Management's Discussion and Analysis for the years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023. The forward-looking statements in this press release are applicable only as of the date of this release or as of the date specified in the relevant forward-looking statement. The Company has no intention and undertakes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by applicable law.

NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION TO U.S. NEWSWIRE SERVICES OR FOR DISSEMINATION IN THE UNITED STATES

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/274658
